questions.question: What is the primary characteristic that distinguishes sequential logic circuits from combinational logic circuits?
options: Sequential circuits have memory and their output depends on current and past inputs, whereas combinational circuits' output depends only on current inputs.
Combinational circuits always require a clock signal to operate correctly.
Sequential circuits use only basic logic gates like AND, OR, and NOT, without any feedback paths.
Combinational circuits can store data indefinitely even without power.
Sequential circuits are inherently faster in processing data compared to combinational circuits of similar complexity.

questions.question: When a cache line needs to be replaced to make room for a new block, which of the following cache eviction policies aims to discard the block that has not been accessed for the longest period of time?
options: Least Recently Used (LRU)
First-In, First-Out (FIFO)
Random Replacement (RR)
Most Recently Used (MRU)
Least Frequently Used (LFU)

questions.question: In the physical design phase of an integrated circuit, what is the primary objective of the 'Placement' step?
options: To convert the Register-Transfer Level (RTL) description into a gate-level netlist.
To assign physical locations on the chip for all standard cells, macros, and I/O pins, while minimizing wire length and meeting timing constraints.
To verify the functional correctness of the design using simulation and formal methods.
To connect all the placed components with actual physical wires (interconnects) and minimize routing congestion.
To verify that the layout adheres to manufacturing design rules specified by the foundry.

questions.question: Which of the following parallel processing architectures is best described by having multiple processing elements that each execute different instructions on different data streams simultaneously?
options: Single Instruction, Multiple Data (SIMD)
Multiple Instruction, Single Data (MISD)
Multiple Instruction, Multiple Data (MIMD)
Single Instruction, Single Data (SISD)
Vector Processor

questions.question: In synchronous digital circuits, which of the following describes the 'setup time' requirement for a flip-flop?
options: The minimum amount of time the data input must remain stable *after* the active clock edge for it to be reliably latched.
The minimum amount of time the data input must be stable *before* the active clock edge for it to be reliably latched.
The maximum propagation delay allowed for the clock signal to reach the flip-flop's clock input.
The time it takes for the flip-flop's output to transition to its new state after the active clock edge.
The interval during which the output of the flip-flop is metastable or indeterminate after a clock transition.
